hero.desc: At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, we treat melasma with calm, step by step plans that respect Asian skin and real life in Malaysia.
hero.glassQuote: "You look in the mirror and the patches are the first thing you see. Cheeks. Upper lip. Forehead. Sometimes all three."

understanding.title: What is melasma?
understanding.desc: Melasma is a type of pigmentation that appears as brown or grey brown patches, usually on the face. It's common, especially in women, and often linked with hormones and sun exposure.
understanding.whyMalaysiaDesc: Year round strong UV levels and hot, humid climate make melasma management uniquely challenging.
understanding.isItDesc: Melasma looks patchy and spread out, appears on both sides of the face, and deepens with triggers.
understanding.isItNote: Can be confused with sunspots, PIH, or freckles — which is why we start with assessment, not assumptions.

approach.title: Why melasma needs a plan, not a single treatment
approach.desc: Most people don't fail treatment. They fail incomplete treatment.
approach.step2Desc: Laser, peel, or both depending on depth

treatments.title: Melasma treatment options at Nexus Clinic
treatments.t1Desc: Target pigment clusters dengan gradual, safe untuk Asian skin
treatments.t2Desc: Professional depigmentation reset untuk melasma stubborn
treatments.t3Desc: Surface pigment treatment dengan barrier protection
treatments.t4Desc: Customized home care untuk long-term control

results.title: What to expect
results.result1: Patches soften and blend into your natural tone
results.result2: Makeup sits better, stop color correcting daily
results.result3: Flare ups become smaller and easier to calm
results.importantDesc: Melasma often improves in stages, not overnight. Dermal or deeper pigment is harder to "erase" — the goal becomes visible softening and long term control.
results.riskDesc: Rebound pigmentation (PIH) if laser is too aggressive. We prioritize controlled progress, not trauma.

prevention.title: Your melasma prevention routine in Malaysia
prevention.tip1Desc: Even when indoors. Melasma is triggered by light.
prevention.tip2Desc: Iron oxide in tinted products helps block visible light.
prevention.tip3Desc: Over-exfoliation is a common mistake. Don't scrub.

faq.title: Common questions about melasma
faq.q1: Is laser safe for melasma on Asian skin?
faq.a1: Yes, with right settings and gradual approach. Aggressive lasers can trigger rebound pigmentation.
faq.q2: How many sessions do I need?
faq.a2: Varies based on depth, skin type, and sun protection consistency. Multiple sessions are normal.
faq.q3: Does melasma come back after treatment?
faq.a3: Relapse is common if triggers return. Maintenance is part of the plan, not an afterthought.
faq.q4: What sunscreen is best for melasma in Malaysia?
faq.a4: Broad spectrum with visible light protection. Tinted products with iron oxide are recommended.

cta.title1: If your melasma keeps coming back,
cta.desc: Most people were never given a full plan. Book a consultation and let's map your pigment, calm your triggers, and build a routine you can actually keep up with.
